data.description:
## Why
Make prompts contract-bearing palette citizens like scripted ops, and represent the single_turn
vs agentic distinction in the IR and runner.

## What
- In `src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py` (RunnerAgentStep), resolve the prompt's declared input/output
  contract + `execution_mode`; surface consumes/produces/description in the inspector (reuse S0's path).
- In `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`, honor `execution_mode`: `single_turn` = ONE model call with ZERO
  tools producing structured output validated against the output contract; `agentic` = the current
  pydantic_ai tool-using path (unchanged).
- `output_contract` change on an override/variant is BREAKING: `validate` flags override-vs-builtin
  contract drift.

## Scope
In: runner mode + prompt-contract surfacing + drift flag. Out: deep/runtime validation (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An agent step surfaces its prompt's contract in the inspector; `single_turn` runs with no tools; `agentic` is unchanged.
- [ ] `validate` flags an override/variant that changes a prompt's `output_contract`.
- [ ] A live single_turn prompt produces structured output validated against its output contract.
data.title: Prompt contracts in IR/runner + execution_mode + inspector surfacing
